title: Learning from Home: The Rise of Online Education
passage:
Learning from Home

Online learning is very popular all over the world today. Many students do not go to a traditional school building anymore. Instead, they stay at home and learn. To study online, you only need a computer, a laptop, or a tablet. You also need a fast internet connection and a quiet room to focus on your lessons.

There are many good things about online learning. First, you can choose your own study time. Some students like to study in the early morning, and others prefer to study in the evening. Second, you do not need to travel to school by bus, train, or car. This saves a lot of time and money. You can also wear comfortable clothes at home while you study.

However, online learning can also have some problems. It is sometimes difficult to study alone because there is no teacher in the room to help you immediately. Some students easily get distracted by video games, mobile phones, or television. Also, you do not see your classmates in person. You might feel lonely because you cannot talk or play with your friends during school breaks.

Let us look at Tom as an example. Tom is a twelve-year-old student from London. He studies online because his family travels to different countries every month. His father is a professional photographer. Tom uses his laptop to watch video lessons every morning. He does his homework on the computer and sends it to his teacher by email. Tom likes online school because he can see beautiful places with his parents. But he also misses playing football with his old school friends.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What does a student need to study online?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 states: 'To study online, you only need a computer, a laptop, or a tablet.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why does online learning save money?

options.B: You do not travel to school.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Second, you do not need to travel to school by bus, train, or car. This saves a lot of time and money.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: Why do some students feel lonely when studying online?

options.C: They cannot play with friends during school breaks.
options.D: They must study in the morning.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'You might feel lonely because you cannot talk or play with your friends during school breaks.'

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

options.A: He does not like traditional schools.
options.C: His family travels to different countries every month.
options.D: He wants to stay in London.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'He studies online because his family travels to different countries every month.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: All students prefer to study in the early morning.

options.TRUE: The statement agrees with the information.
options.NOT GIVEN: There is no information on this.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'Some students like to study in the early morning, and others prefer to study in the evening.' Therefore, not all students prefer the morning.

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: It is sometimes difficult to study alone at home.

options.TRUE: The statement agrees with the information.
options.NOT GIVEN: There is no information on this.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'It is sometimes difficult to study alone because there is no teacher in the room to help you immediately.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.
questions.text: Tom's father is a professional photographer.

options.TRUE: The statement agrees with the information.
options.NOT GIVEN: There is no information on this.

questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'His father is a professional photographer.'

questions.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.

options.TRUE: The statement agrees with the information.
options.NOT GIVEN: There is no information on this.

questions.explanation: The text mentions Tom is from London, but it does not mention where his teacher lives.

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Online students can wear ________ clothes at home.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 states: 'You can also wear comfortable clothes at home while you study.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Students can get distracted by video games, television, or ________.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 states: 'Some students easily get distracted by video games, mobile phones, or television.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Tom is a ________ student from London.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'Tom is a twelve-year-old student from London.'

questions.instruction: Complete the sentences below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.text: Tom sends his homework to his teacher by ________.
questions.explanation: Paragraph 4 states: 'He does his homework on the computer and sends it to his teacher by email.'

vocabulary.meaning: following ideas or methods that have existed for a long time
making you feel physically relaxed; pleasant to wear
not able to concentrate or pay attention to something
unhappy because you are alone or do not have friends
doing a job for money as a main career

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài đạt điểm cao (A1-A2)

1. Multiple Choice: Đọc kỹ từ khóa trong câu hỏi. Ví dụ: 'What does a student need...'. Sau đó, quét nhanh (scanning) trong bài để tìm các từ liên quan như 'need', 'study online'. Bạn sẽ thấy đáp án nằm ngay ở đoạn đầu tiên.

2. True/False/Not Given: Chú ý các từ chỉ tần suất hoặc số lượng tuyệt đối như 'All', 'Always', 'Sometimes'. Trong câu 5, đề bài ghi 'All students' nhưng trong bài chỉ nói 'Some students', đây là bẫy phổ biến dẫn đến câu trả lời là FALSE.

3. Sentence Completion: Đọc kỹ giới hạn từ cho phép (NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS). Tìm câu có cấu trúc tương tự trong bài và chép chính xác từ vựng từ bài đọc, không tự ý thay đổi dạng từ (ví dụ: không đổi từ số nhiều sang số ít hoặc ngược lại).
